gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/236)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/236)
text: رِوَايَةٍ أُخْرَى عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي الْمَوْلُودِ لَهُ مَا لِلرِّجَالِ وَ لَهُ مَا لِلنِّسَاءِ يَبُولُ مِنْهُمَا جَمِيعاً قَالَ مِنْ أَيِّهِمَا سَبَقَ قِيلَ فَإِنْ خَرَجَ مِنْهُمَا جَمِيعاً قَالَ فَمِنْ أَيِّهِمَا اسْتَدَرَّ قِيلَ فَإِنِ اسْتَدَرَّا جَمِيعاً قَالَ فَمِنْ أَبْعَدِهِمَا .

en.hubeali: (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the child born for him being what is for the men and for him being what is for the women, urinating from both parts together. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘From whichever it (urine) preceded’. It was said, ‘So if it came out from both parts together?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So from whichever of the two it gushes out’. It was said, ‘So if it were to gush out from both of them together?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So from the one it reaches the furthest’.
en.sarwar: 5. This is in another Hadith from abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a person who has the reproductive organs of both male and female and urinates from both of them at the same time: ‘He (the Imam) said that it is according to that from which urine comes first and if it is equal in this respect he (the Imam) said that it then is according to that which stops last and if this also is equal than it is according to that which sends farther/”
